message.content: {"items":[{"title":"European Community Household Panel (ECHP), Waves 1-8, 1994-2001","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA3640","justification":"Multi-country panel including Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Netherlands, Portugal, Spain, Great Britain; 1990s waves cover employment, firm characteristics via worker context, income and economic conditions relevant to business/industrial organisation and economic development."},{"title":"Eurobarometer 39.0 (March–April 1993): Internal Market and Expectations of the European Community","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA2043","justification":"Covers EC member states including Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Netherlands, Portugal, Spain, Great Britain; focuses on internal market, competition, consumer and business expectations, directly tied to economic systems and integration in the early 1990s."},{"title":"Eurobarometer 41.0 (March–April 1994): Trade and Commerce, Single Market, Public Procurement","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA2485","justification":"Addresses trade, commerce, single market and public procurement in EU member states including target Western European countries; central to business environment, industrial organisation, and economic system integration during the 1990s."},{"title":"Eurobarometer 47.0 (March–April 1997): The Single Market and the European Currency","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA2906","justification":"Includes Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Luxembourg, Netherlands, Portugal, Spain, Great Britain; questions about the single market and EMU relate to firms' operating environment, competition, and economic system development in the late 1990s."},{"title":"Eurobarometer 50.1 (October–November 1998): Business in the Single Market","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA3069","justification":"Explicitly focuses on business in the EU single market; includes many of the requested Western European countries and examines company activities, competition, cross-border trade and regulatory environment, fitting ‘business/industrial management and organisation’ and ‘economic systems and development’ in the late 1990s."},{"title":"Eurobarometer 52.0 (November–December 1999): The Single Market, Financial Services, and Small and Medium Enterprises","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA3200","justification":"Concentrates on SMEs, single market and financial services across EU member states including requested Western European countries; directly aligned with business organisation, SME development and broader economic system functioning at the end of the 1990s."},{"title":"European Company Survey 1993 (ECS 1993) – Manufacturing and Services in the European Community","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA2623","justification":"Firm-level survey (management-side) on work organisation, human resource practices and industrial relations in manufacturing and services; includes core EU15 countries such as Belgium, Germany, Denmark, France, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Netherlands, Portugal, Spain, Great Britain; squarely about industrial management and organisation in the early 1990s."},{"title":"European Company Survey 1996 (ECS 1996) – Organisation of Work and Employment in Europe","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA3063","justification":"Covers establishments in several EU countries including Germany, France, Italy, Netherlands, Spain, Great Britain; examines organisational change, work organisation and employment practices, central to business/industrial management and organisational structures in mid-1990s."},{"title":"European Social Survey Prototype 1999 (ESS Pilot) – Economic and Social Change in Europe","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA3910","justification":"Pilot cross-national survey including several Western European countries; contains modules on perceptions of economic performance, labour market, and institutions, relevant to understanding economic systems and development context for businesses in the late 1990s."},{"title":"European Community Innovation Survey (CIS-2, 1994–1996) – Community Innovation Survey II","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA3432","justification":"Firm-level data on innovation activities in manufacturing and services across EU member states including Belgium, Germany, Denmark, France, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Netherlands, Portugal, Spain, Great Britain; directly tied to business organisation, R&D, and structural economic development in the mid-1990s."}]}
